what: Visualize the data and point outputs
title: NOAA North American Regional Reanalysis (NARR)
text: plot NARR 2m air temperature Uber H3 hexagons. NARR fairly coarse spatial resolution ~32sq-km, don’t see much spatial variability, facets show daily trend
code: usa <- sf::st_as_sf(maps::map("usa", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE)) bb <- sf::st_bbox(usa)  # Project first layer to WGS84 and convert to data frame for safe ggplot2 rendering lyr1 <- terra::project(processed_data[[1]], "EPSG:4326") lyr1_df <- as.data.frame(lyr1, xy = TRUE) names(lyr1_df)[3] <- "value"  ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_raster(     data = lyr1_df,     ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y, fill = value)   ) +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = usa, fill = NA, color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", na.value = "white") +   ggplot2::coord_sf(xlim = bb[c(1, 3)], ylim = bb[c(2, 4)], expand = FALSE) +   ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "air temp at 2m (K)") +   ggplot2::theme_minimal() ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::geom_sf(data = hex_values, ggplot2::aes(fill = air.2m_0)) + ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c() + ggplot2::facet_wrap(~time)

title: NOAA North American Regional Reanalysis (NARR)
text: download request now covers snow water equivalent (weasd) 2m air temperature (air.2m). NARR uses projected source data internally, vignette leaves reprojection amadeus keeps extraction geometry WGS84 input coordinates.

title: US EPA National Emissions Inventory (NEI)
text: download_nei() intentionally narrow currently targets EPA -road NEI summary releases: year main selector currently supports two NEI releases wired amadeus: 2017 2020. Requests can include one year available years, yielding one annual -road summary file per year requested. NEI downloads arrive zip archives can unzipped automatically unzip = TRUE; workflow processes extracted annual summary tables. jurisdiction argument needed source files EPA -region summaries rather state--state download variants. NEI require authentication. Temporal resolution annual.

title: US EPA National Emissions Inventory (NEI)
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "nei_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "nei",   year = 2020L,   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE )

title: US EPA National Emissions Inventory (NEI)
code: county <- tigris::counties("NC", year = 2020, cb = TRUE, class = "sf") data_path = paste0(directory_to_save, "/data_files/2020nei_onroad_byregion/") county$FIPS <- county$GEOID processed_data <- process_covariates(   covariate = "nei",   path = data_path,   county = county,   year = 2020 )

title: US EPA National Emissions Inventory (NEI)
code: domain_x <- c(terra::xmin(processed_data), terra::xmax(processed_data)) domain_y <- c(terra::ymin(processed_data), terra::ymax(processed_data)) domain_dx <- diff(domain_x) domain_dy <- diff(domain_y)  candidate_xy <- expand.grid(   lon = seq(domain_x[1] + 0.12 * domain_dx, domain_x[2] - 0.12 * domain_dx, length.out = 5),   lat = seq(domain_y[1] + 0.12 * domain_dy, domain_y[2] - 0.12 * domain_dy, length.out = 5) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   candidate_xy,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 ) example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))    point_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "nei",   from = processed_data,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   geom = "sf" )  print(point_values, n = 25)

what: Visualize the point and polygon outputs
title: US EPA National Emissions Inventory (NEI)
code: ggplot() + geom_sf(data = county, color = NA) +   geom_sf(data = point_values, aes(color = TRF_NEINP_0_00000), size = 2) +   labs(title = "NEI On-Road Emissions by County (2020)", color = "Total Emissions") +   theme_minimal()

title: MRLC National Land Cover Database (NLCD)
text: download_data(dataset_name = "nlcd", ...) accepts product values "Land Cover", "Land Cover Change", "Land Cover Confidence", "Fractional Impervious Surface", "Impervious Descriptor", "Spectral Change Day Year". NLCD nationwide U.S. product 30 m resolution, year values available 1985 2024. Downloads arrive zipped MRLC raster bundles. Interpretation depends product: land-cover-style layers class/code rasters, fractional impervious surface continuous percent-style surface, spectral change day year encodes timing detected change.

title: MRLC National Land Cover Database (NLCD)
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "nlcd_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "nlcd",   product = "Land Cover",   year = 2021,   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE )

title: MRLC National Land Cover Database (NLCD)
code: data_dir <- paste0(directory_to_save, "/data_files")  processed_data <- process_covariates(   covariate = "nlcd",   path = data_dir,   year = 2021 )

title: MRLC National Land Cover Database (NLCD)
code: example_points_terra <- terra::spatSample(    processed_data[[1]],    size = 25,    method = "regular",    as.points = TRUE,    na.rm = TRUE,    values = FALSE  )  example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(example_points_terra)  example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))   point_values <- calculate_covariates(    covariate = "nlcd",    from = processed_data,    locs = example_points_sf,    locs_id = "site_id",    mode = "terra",    radius = 1000,    geom = "sf",    drop = FALSE  )  print(point_values, n = 25)

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
text: GOES ADP geostationary satellite aerosol detection product reports smoke dust presence ABI observations.

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
text: download_data(dataset_name = "goes", ...) wraps download_goes(). satellite accepts "16" (GOES-East) "18" (GOES-West). product accepts "ADP-C" (CONUS), "ADP-F" (Full Disk), "ADP-M" (Mesoscale). date accepts either single day start/end range YYYY-MM-DD format. GOES ADP require authentication. process_covariates(covariate = "goes", ...) supports variable = "Smoke" "Dust".

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "goes_workflow") download_goes(   date = c("2024-01-01","2024-01-01"),   satellite = "16",   product = "ADP-C",   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE )

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
code: goes_path <- file.path(directory_to_save, "ABI-L2-ADPC", "2024", "001")  processed_mean <- process_covariates(   covariate = "goes",   variable = "Smoke",   date = "2024-01-01",   path = goes_path,   daily_agg = TRUE,   fun = "mean" )  processed_sum <- process_covariates(   covariate = "goes",   variable = "Smoke",   date = "2024-01-01",   path = goes_path,   daily_agg = TRUE,   fun = "sum" )

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
text: Use fun = "mean" want daily field behaves like fractional probability-like surface. GOES Smoke/Dust, mean usually better default exposure-oriented summaries represents typical share available granules indicating smoke dust pixel day. Use fun = "sum" want integrate granule-level detections count-like daily total. can useful identifying places repeated detections across day, coarse approximation GOES granule cadence varies sector, satellite operations, data availability. epidemiologic environmental exposure workflows, start fun = "mean" unless analysis specifically needs detection-frequency metric. Computational notes: memory, runtime, parallel-processing guidance, see vignette("computational_considerations", package = "amadeus").

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
code: domain_x <- c(terra::xmin(processed_mean), terra::xmax(processed_mean)) domain_y <- c(terra::ymin(processed_mean), terra::ymax(processed_mean)) domain_dx <- diff(domain_x) domain_dy <- diff(domain_y)  candidate_xy <- expand.grid(   lon = seq(domain_x[1] + 0.12 * domain_dx, domain_x[2] - 0.12 * domain_dx, length.out = 5),   lat = seq(domain_y[1] + 0.12 * domain_dy, domain_y[2] - 0.12 * domain_dy, length.out = 5) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   candidate_xy,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 ) example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))  point_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "goes",   from = processed_mean,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 0,   fun = "mean",   geom = "sf" )

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
code: smoke_col <- grep("^Smoke_", names(point_values), value = TRUE)[1] if (is.na(smoke_col) || smoke_col == "") {   excluded <- c("site_id", "h3_id", attr(point_values, "sf_column"))   fallback_cols <- setdiff(names(point_values), excluded)   fallback_numeric <- fallback_cols[     vapply(point_values[fallback_cols], is.numeric, logical(1))   ]   smoke_col <- fallback_numeric[1] }  ggplot(data = point_values) +   geom_sf(aes(color = .data[[smoke_col]])) +   ggtitle("Daily GOES Smoke values")

title: NOAA GOES Aerosol Detection Product (ADP)
text: processed daily raster can summarized polygon features. example builds two small synthetic rectangles processed raster extent extracts mean daily Smoke value area.
code: poly_x <- domain_x[1] + c(0.20, 0.35, 0.50) * domain_dx poly_y <- domain_y[1] + c(0.20, 0.40, 0.60) * domain_dy make_rect <- function(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) {   sf::st_polygon(list(rbind(     c(xmin, ymin),     c(xmax, ymin),     c(xmax, ymax),     c(xmin, ymax),     c(xmin, ymin)   ))) }  example_polys_sf <- sf::st_sf(   area_id = c("area_1", "area_2"),   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     make_rect(poly_x[1], poly_x[2], poly_y[1], poly_y[2]),     make_rect(poly_x[2], poly_x[3], poly_y[2], poly_y[3]),     crs = 4326   ) )  polygon_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "goes",   from = processed_mean,   locs = example_polys_sf,   locs_id = "area_id",   radius = 0,   fun = "mean",   geom = "sf" ) polygon_values

title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
text: download_aqs() download_improve() expose key availability choices PM network downloads: parameter_code selects EPA pollutant parameter. Common examples documented amadeus include PM2.5 (88101 88502), PM10 (81102), ozone (44201), NO2 (42602), SO2 (42401), CO (42101). resolution_temporal currently supports "daily", downloadable files daily monitor observations rather hourly annual summaries. year accepts single year start/end pair, downloading one pre-generated EPA archive per year requested. AQS downloads arrive zipped annual files can unzipped automatically unzip = TRUE; workflow reads extracted daily CSV files. IMPROVE supports product = "raw", "rhr2", "rhr3"; workflow uses "raw" filters ParamCode == "FPM" (fine particulate mass, PM2.5 indicator). Neither AQS IMPROVE requires authentication, intended outcome modeling workflows.

what: AQS parameters used in this example
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: aqs_parameters      pollutant parameter_code 1        PM2.5          88101 2 PM2.5-NonFRM          88502 3          NO2          42602

what: IMPROVE parameters used in this example
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: improve_parameters   pollutant product parameter_code 1     PM2.5     raw             MF

what: Download PM2.5 and NO2 daily AQS data
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: aqs_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "aqs_workflow")  for (i in seq_len(nrow(aqs_parameters))) {   download_aqs(     parameter_code = aqs_parameters$parameter_code[i],     resolution_temporal = "daily",     year = 2022,     directory_to_save = aqs_dir,     acknowledgement = TRUE,     unzip = TRUE,     remove_zip = FALSE   ) }

what: Process PM2.5 and NO2 with process_aqs()
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: aqs_processed <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(aqs_parameters)), function(i) {   csv_path <- file.path(     aqs_dir,     "data_files",     sprintf("daily_%s_2022.csv", aqs_parameters$parameter_code[i])   )    locations <- process_aqs(     path = csv_path,     date = aqs_date_window,     mode = "location",     return_format = "sf"   )   locations$pollutant <- aqs_parameters$pollutant[i]    daily_data <- process_aqs(     path = csv_path,     date = aqs_date_window,     mode = "available-data",     return_format = "data.table"   )   daily_data[, pollutant := aqs_parameters$pollutant[i]]    list(     locations = locations,     daily = daily_data   ) })

what: Plot monitor locations with facets for PM2.5 and NO2
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: aqs_locations <- do.call(   rbind,   lapply(aqs_processed, `[[`, "locations") )  aqs_time_series <- data.table::rbindlist(   lapply(aqs_processed, `[[`, "daily"),   fill = TRUE ) ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = aqs_locations, color = "#0072B2", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ pollutant, ncol = 2) +   ggplot2::coord_sf(datum = NA) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "AQS monitor locations for PM2.5 and NO2",     subtitle = paste(aqs_date_window[1], "to", aqs_date_window[2]),     x = NULL,     y = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal() +   ggplot2::theme(     panel.spacing = grid::unit(1, "lines")   )

what: Show an example time series of the downloaded AQS data
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: aqs_time_series[, time := as.Date(time)]  example_sites <- aqs_time_series[   ,   .N,   by = .(pollutant, site_id) ][   order(pollutant, -N, site_id) ][   ,   .SD[1],   by = pollutant ]  aqs_time_series <- merge(   aqs_time_series,   example_sites[, .(pollutant, site_id)],   by = c("pollutant", "site_id") ) ggplot2::ggplot(   aqs_time_series,   ggplot2::aes(x = time, y = Arithmetic.Mean, group = site_id) ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(color = "#D55E00", linewidth = 0.5) +   ggplot2::geom_point(color = "#D55E00", size = 0.8) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ pollutant, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +   ggplot2::scale_x_date(     date_breaks = "2 weeks",     date_labels = "%b %d"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Example AQS daily time series",     subtitle = "One site per pollutant, chosen from monitors with available observations in this date window",     x = "Date",     y = "Arithmetic mean"   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal() +   ggplot2::theme(     panel.spacing = grid::unit(1, "lines"),     axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)   )

what: Download IMPROVE daily PM2.5 data (FPM, raw product)
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: improve_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "improve_workflow")  download_improve(   year = 2022,   product = improve_parameters$product[1],   directory_to_save = improve_dir,   acknowledgement = TRUE )

title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: improve_all <- process_improve(   path = improve_dir,   product = improve_parameters$product[1],   date = improve_date_window,   return_format = "data.table" )  improve_pm25 <- improve_all[   ParamCode == improve_parameters$parameter_code[1] & Status == "V0" ]  improve_locations_raw <- process_improve(   path = improve_dir,   product = improve_parameters$product[1],   date = improve_date_window,   return_format = "sf" )  improve_locations <- unique(   improve_locations_raw[     improve_locations_raw$ParamCode == improve_parameters$parameter_code[1] &       improve_locations_raw$Status == "V0",   ][, c("SiteCode", "ParamCode", "Status")] )

title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = improve_locations, color = "#009E73", size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +   ggplot2::coord_sf(datum = NA) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "IMPROVE PM2.5 (FPM) monitor locations",     subtitle = paste(improve_date_window[1], "to", improve_date_window[2]),     x = NULL,     y = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal()

what: Show an example IMPROVE PM2.5 time series
title: Particulate Matter (PM) data network workflows
code: improve_pm25[, FactDate := as.Date(FactDate)]  example_improve_site <- improve_pm25[   ,   .N,   by = SiteCode ][   order(-N, SiteCode) ][1, SiteCode]  improve_example_ts <- improve_pm25[SiteCode == example_improve_site] ggplot2::ggplot(   improve_example_ts,   ggplot2::aes(x = FactDate, y = FactValue, group = SiteCode) ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(color = "#0072B2", linewidth = 0.5) +   ggplot2::geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 0.8) +   ggplot2::scale_x_date(     date_breaks = "2 weeks",     date_labels = "%b %d"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Example IMPROVE PM2.5 (FPM) time series",     subtitle = paste("Site:", example_improve_site),     x = "Date",     y = "PM2.5 mass concentration (ug/m^3)"   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal() +   ggplot2::theme(     axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)   )

title: NASA SEDAC Population Density
text: download_data(dataset_name = "sedac_population", ...) wraps download_population(). Inputs global GPW population-density rasters keyed year; request single year year = "" -years product. data_resolution supports "30 second", "2.5 minute", "15 minute", "30 minute", "60 minute". -years product available 30-second resolution, amadeus automatically falls back 2.5-minute resolution case. data_format accepts "GeoTIFF", "ASCII", "netCDF". -years product available netCDF downloaded way even another format requested. Downloads global rather extent-based; crop study area processing. Files arrive zip archives, unzip remove_zip controlling archive handling. Access may require NASA EarthData authentication, amadeus use NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN needed.

title: NASA SEDAC Population Density
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "population_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "sedac_population",   year = "2020",   data_format = "GeoTIFF",   data_resolution = "15 minute",   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE )

title: NASA SEDAC Population Density
code: processed_data <- process_covariates(   covariate = "population",   path = list.files(     directory_to_save,     pattern = "\\.tif$",     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   )[1] ) plot(log10(processed_data))

title: NASA SEDAC Population Density
code: df <- data.frame(   site_id = c("site_1", "site_2", "site_3","site_4"),   lon = c(-157.8583, -155.5319, -155.5828,-159.5),   lat = c(21.3069, 19.5, 19.8968, 22.0) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   df,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 )   point_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "population",   from = processed_data,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 1000,   fun = "mean",   geom = "sf" )  print(point_values)

title: PRISM Climate Data
text: download_data(dataset_name = "prism", ...) combines time, element, data_type. Historical PRISM bundles also accept time = "YYYY" years 1895-1980 return zip file containing 12 monthly grids plus annual grid. format used data_type = "ts" can nc, asc, grib2. sol* elements available normals , ts. normals, format ignored. PRISM API always returns zip file, even time-series format requested.

title: PRISM Climate Data
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "prism_ts_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "prism",   time = "201005",   element = "tmean",   data_type = "ts",   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE )

title: PRISM Climate Data
code: processed_data <- process_covariates(   covariate = "prism",   path = list.files(     paste0(directory_to_save,"/data_files/"),     pattern = ".nc",     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   )[1],   element = "tmean",   time = "201005",   extent = terra::ext(-114.9, -102.0, 31.3, 41.1) )

title: PRISM Climate Data
code: domain_x <- c(terra::xmin(processed_data), terra::xmax(processed_data)) domain_y <- c(terra::ymin(processed_data), terra::ymax(processed_data)) domain_dx <- diff(domain_x) domain_dy <- diff(domain_y)  candidate_xy <- expand.grid(   lon = seq(domain_x[1] + 0.12 * domain_dx, domain_x[2] - 0.12 * domain_dx, length.out = 5),   lat = seq(domain_y[1] + 0.12 * domain_dy, domain_y[2] - 0.12 * domain_dy, length.out = 5) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   candidate_xy,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 ) example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))   point_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "prism",   from = processed_data,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 1000,   geom = "sf" )

title: PRISM Climate Data
code: point_basemap <-   sf::st_as_sf(maps::map("world", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))  ggplot() +     geom_sf(data = point_basemap, fill = "gray80", color = "white") +   geom_sf(data = point_values, aes(color = tmean_1000), size = 3) +   scale_color_viridis_c() + labs(title = "PRISM tmean at example points") +   coord_sf(xlim = domain_x, ylim = domain_y)

title: Protected Data Sources
text: vignette demonstrate create NASA EarthData Account, generate personal access token, configure amadeus authenticate automatically using token.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: Visit https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/ register log NASA EarthData account. Account registration provides access NASA’s Earth Observing System Data Information System (EOSDIS) twelve Distributed Active Archive Centers (DAAC), including: Alaska Satellite Facility (ASF) DAAC Atmospheric Science Data Center (ASDC) Crustal Dynamics Data Information System (CDDIS) Global Hydrometeorology Resource Center (GHRC) Goddard Earth Sciences Data Information Services Center (GES DISC) Land Processes DAAC (LP DAAC) Level 1 Atmosphere Archive Distribution System (LAADS) DAAC National Snow Ice Data Center (NSIDC) DAAC Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) DAAC Ocean Biology DAAC (OB.DAAC) Physical Oceanography DAAC (PO.DAAC) Socioeconomic Data Applications Center (SEDAC) See https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/eosdis/daacs information.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: creating account, navigate “Profile” (https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/profile), “Applications > Authorized Apps”. “Authorized Apps” page specifies NASA EarthData applications can use login credentials. example, ensure authorization enabled “SEDAC Website”, “SEDAC Website (Alpha)”, “SEDAC Website (Beta)”.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: NASA EarthData account set required applications authorized, generate personal access token use amadeus. Log https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/ Navigate “Profile” → “Generate Token” Click “Generate Token” copy resulting token string Tokens expire 90 days. Repeat steps generate new token needed.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: amadeus provides setup_nasa_token() securely store token available every R session without ever appearing scripts version-controlled files.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: running command, restart R (run readRenviron("~/.Renviron")) environment variable NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN take effect. Subsequent R sessions pick token automatically. Alternatively, pass token directly:
code: # Interactively prompts for the token, then writes to ~/.Renviron setup_nasa_token(method = "renviron") setup_nasa_token(method = "renviron", token = "your_token_here")

title: Protected Data Sources
text: Pass file path individual download functions:
code: # Saves token to ~/.nasa_earthdata_token (permissions set to user-only) setup_nasa_token(method = "file", token = "your_token_here") download_data(   dataset_name = "sedac_population",   year = "2020",   data_format = "GeoTIFF",   data_resolution = "60 minute",   directory_to_save = "./sedac_population",   acknowledgement = TRUE,   nasa_earth_data_token = "~/.nasa_earthdata_token" )

title: Protected Data Sources
text: set environment variable manually:
code: # Sets the token for the current R session only (lost on exit) setup_nasa_token(method = "session", token = "your_token_here") Sys.setenv(NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN = "your_token_here")

title: Protected Data Sources
text: NASA download functions (MERRA-2, MODIS, GEOS-CF, SEDAC population, SEDAC groads) call get_token() internally. lookup priority : NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN environment variable (set via ~/.Renviron Sys.setenv()) — recommended File path passed nasa_earth_data_token argument Token string passed directly nasa_earth_data_token argument (recommended scripts) token configured ~/.Renviron, extra arguments needed:
code: # NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN is read automatically from the environment download_data(   dataset_name = "sedac_population",   year = "2020",   data_format = "GeoTIFF",   data_resolution = "60 minute",   directory_to_save = "./sedac_population",   acknowledgement = TRUE ) ## Using token from environment variable: NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN ## Downloading requested files... ## Requested files have been downloaded. ## Unzipping files... ## Files unzipped and saved in ./sedac_population/.

title: Protected Data Sources
text: EOSDIS Distributed Active Archive Centers (DAAC). National Aeronautics Space Administration (NASA). Date accessed: January 3, 2024. https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/eosdis/daacs. NASA EarthData Token Documentation. National Aeronautics Space Administration (NASA). https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov/documentation/for_users/user_token.

title: Climatology Lab TerraClimate
text: download_data(dataset_name = "terraclimate", ...) accepts either full variable names TerraClimate codes. Temporal resolution: monthly; download annual NetCDF file containing monthly layers one variable. Year input: use single year start/end pair c(2018, 2022). Availability check: wrapper validates first requested variable-year URL stops request returns HTTP 404. Major constraint: TerraClimate downloads require authentication.

title: Climatology Lab TerraClimate
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "terraclimate_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "terraclimate",   variables = c("Precipitation"),   year = 2019,   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE )

title: Climatology Lab TerraClimate
code: processed_data <- process_covariates(   covariate = "terraclimate",   variable = "ppt",   date = c("2019-01-01", "2019-02-01"),   path = dirname(list.files(     directory_to_save,     pattern = "\\.nc$",     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   )[1]),   extent = terra::ext(-114.9, -102.0, 31.3, 41.1) )  plot(processed_data, main = "TerraClimate ppt for Jan-Feb 2019")

title: Climatology Lab TerraClimate
code: domain_x <- c(terra::xmin(processed_data), terra::xmax(processed_data)) domain_y <- c(terra::ymin(processed_data), terra::ymax(processed_data)) domain_dx <- diff(domain_x) domain_dy <- diff(domain_y)  candidate_xy <- expand.grid(   lon = seq(domain_x[1] + 0.12 * domain_dx, domain_x[2] - 0.12 * domain_dx, length.out = 5),   lat = seq(domain_y[1] + 0.12 * domain_dy, domain_y[2] - 0.12 * domain_dy, length.out = 5) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   candidate_xy,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 ) example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))   point_values <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "terraclimate",   from = processed_data,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 0,   fun = "mean",   geom = "sf" )  print(point_values)

what: Workflow for an annual average covariate
title: Climatology Lab TerraClimate
code: annual_process <- process_covariates(   covariate = "terraclimate",   variable = "ppt",   date = c("2019-01-01", "2019-12-31"),   path = dirname(list.files(     directory_to_save,     pattern = "\\.nc$",     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   )[1]),   extent = terra::ext(-114.9, -102.0, 31.3, 41.1) )  point_year <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "terraclimate",   from = annual_process,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 0,   .by_time = "year",   fun = "mean",   geom = "sf" )  print(point_year)

title: US EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI)
text: download_tri() works EPA’s annual TRI basic data files exposes small set high-value selectors: jurisdiction supports nationwide file ("US"), two-letter state territory code "NC", tribal file ("tbl"). year accepts single year start/end pair, multi-year requests download one annual TRI file per year. TRI downloads delivered directly CSV files rather zip archives. Output names reflect jurisdiction requested: U.S.-wide files keep historical tri_raw_<year>.csv pattern, state tribal requests append jurisdiction suffix _NC _tbl. TRI require authentication. annual facility-reported releases waste-management totals, temporal resolution yearly.

title: US EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI)
code: directory_to_save <- file.path(tempdir(), "tri_workflow") download_data(   dataset_name = "tri",   year = 2023L,   jurisdiction = "US",   directory_to_save = directory_to_save,   acknowledgement = TRUE )

what: Demonstate processing and covariate calculation for a single chemical
title: US EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI)
text: helper function get_tri_info() lists available chemicals downloaded files, can used filter processing covariate calculation steps. demonstrate Polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), group persistent organic pollutants widely used industrial applications.
code: chems <- get_tri_info(path = directory_to_save, type = "chemicals")  processed_pcb <- process_covariates(   covariate = "tri",   path = directory_to_save,   chemical = c("Polychlorinated biphenyls"),   year = 2023 ) # Note that extent is an option in process_covariates() to limit the domain

title: US EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI)
code: domain_x <- c(terra::xmin(processed_pcb), terra::xmax(processed_pcb)) domain_y <- c(terra::ymin(processed_pcb), terra::ymax(processed_pcb)) domain_dx <- diff(domain_x) domain_dy <- diff(domain_y)  candidate_xy <- expand.grid(   lon = seq(domain_x[1] + 0.12 * domain_dx, domain_x[2] - 0.12 * domain_dx, length.out = 5),   lat = seq(domain_y[1] + 0.12 * domain_dy, domain_y[2] - 0.12 * domain_dy, length.out = 5) ) example_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(   candidate_xy,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326 ) example_points_sf$site_id <- paste0("site_", seq_len(nrow(example_points_sf)))   point_values_pcb <- calculate_covariates(   covariate = "tri",   from = processed_pcb,   locs = example_points_sf,   locs_id = "site_id",   decay_range = 5000, # 5 km decay range for illustrative purposes   use_threshold = FALSE,   geom = "sf" )

what: Plot the covariates at points along with the facility locations
title: US EPA Toxic Release Inventory (TRI)
code: pcb_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(processed_pcb) point_basemap <-   sf::st_as_sf(maps::map("usa", plot = FALSE, fill = FALSE))  ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = point_basemap, fill = "gray80", color = "white") +   geom_sf(data = pcb_sf, color = "red", size = 2) +    geom_sf(data = point_values_pcb, aes(color = STACK_AIR_0001336363_05000), size = 3)

title: Accessing and Analyzing Large-Scale Environmental Data
text: download_data accesses downloads raw geospatial data variety open source data repositories. function wrapper calls source-specific download functions, account source’s unique combination URL, file naming conventions, data types. Download functions cover following sources: See “download_data” vignette detailed description source-specific download functions. TRI, download_tri() can retrieve EPA annual basic data files nationwide dataset (jurisdiction = "US"), individual states territories (jurisdiction = "AZ", "NC", etc.), tribal file (jurisdiction = "tbl").

title: Accessing and Analyzing Large-Scale Environmental Data
text: Many NASA-hosted datasets require Earthdata Login bearer token. amadeus, includes modis, merra2, geos, population (NASA SEDAC). Use setup_nasa_token() store token calling corresponding download_*() functions. See vignette("protected_datasets", package = "amadeus") detail. setup_nasa_token() supports three storage methods: method = "renviron" writes NASA_EARTHDATA_TOKEN ~/.Renviron persistent personal use; method = "file" writes local token file ~/.nasa_earthdata_token; method = "session" uses Sys.setenv() current R session . Never commit Earthdata tokens git include shared scripts. Prefer method = "renviron" personal machines, method = "session" shared systems CI jobs token supplied CI secret. Example use download_data using NOAA NCEP North American Regional Reanalysis’s (NARR) “weasd” (Daily Accumulated Snow Surface) variable.
code: setup_nasa_token()                              # prompts interactively setup_nasa_token(method = "renviron", token = "<your_token>") directory <- "/  EXAMPLE  /  FILE  /  PATH  /" download_data(   dataset_name = "narr",   year = 2022,   variable = "weasd",   directory_to_save = directory,   acknowledgement = TRUE,   download = TRUE,   hash = TRUE ) Downloading requested files... Requested files have been downloaded. [1] "5655d4281b76f4d4d5bee234c2938f720cfec879" list.files(file.path(directory, "weasd")) [1] "weasd.2022.nc"

title: Perform covariate extraction — calc_worker
text: dataset character(1). Dataset name. SpatRaster(1). Cleaned SpatRaster object. locs_vector SpatVector(1). Cleaned SpatVector object passed calc_prepare_locs(). Contains location point/polygon values. locs_df data.frame(1). Cleaned data.frame object passed calc_prepare_locs(). Contains location identifiers. fun character(1). Summary function. Passed terra::extract(). variable integer. Position within layer name containing variable name/code. time integer. Position within layer name containing time value(s). time_type character(1). Type time observation. One "date", "hour", "year", "yearmonth", "timeless". radius integer(1). Buffer distance (m). Passed calc_prepare_locs(). Used column naming. level integer. Position within layer name containing vertical pressure level value (applicable). Default = NULL. max_cells integer(1). Maximum number cells read . Higher values expedite processing, increase memory usage. Maximum possible value 2^31 - 1. See exactextractr::exact_extract details. weights NULL, SpatRaster, polygon SpatVector/sf, file path. Optional weighting surface used weighted extraction. ... Placeholders.

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
text: calculate_drought() function extracts drought index values point locations object returned process_drought(). Three source datasets supported: SPEI / EDDI (SpatRaster): cell values extracted location using standard raster-extraction pipeline (calc_prepare_locs() → calc_worker() → calc_return_locs()).  Time column format "YYYY-MM-DD". USDM (SpatVector polygons): drought monitor class (DM, integer 0–4) location determined via spatial overlay. time column class Date populated date attribute . .by_time supplied extracted result passed calc_summarize_by() using semantics calculate_*() functions package.

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
code: calculate_drought(   from,   locs,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 0L,   fun = "mean",   weights = NULL,   geom = FALSE,   .by_time = NULL,   ... )

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
text: SpatRaster SpatVector. Output process_drought(). SpatRaster SPEI EDDI sources. SpatVector (polygons) USDM source. locs data.frame, character (path CSV), SpatVector, sf object. Point locations extract values. locs_id character(1). Name unique location identifier column locs. Default "site_id". radius integer(1). Circular buffer radius metres around site location used extraction. SPEI/EDDI controls raster buffering; USDM, radius > 0 additionally returns class proportions within buffer. Default 0L. fun character(1). Summary function applied raster cells within buffer (SPEI/EDDI ). Default "mean". weights NULL, SpatRaster, polygon SpatVector/sf, file path. Optional weights raster weighted extraction. NULL (default), unweighted extraction performed. geom FALSE, "sf", "terra". Whether attach geometry returned object. Default FALSE. .by_time NULL character(1). Name time column use temporal summarization unit token. NULL disables "time". ... Reserved future use; currently ignored.

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
text: data.frame (default) SpatVector/sf object (geom set) columns: <locs_id> Location identifier. time Date observation (Date "YYYY-MM-DD" character). <value_column> Extracted drought index class value. .by_time non-NULL, rows aggregated specified resolution via calc_summarize_by().

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
text: column name extracted drought values follows pattern "<source>_<timescale>_<radius>" (e.g. "spei_01_0") SPEI/EDDI, "usdm_dm_0" USDM. USDM radius > 0, proportion columns added "usdm_dm_<class>_<radius>" classes 0–4.

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
text: Insang Song

title: Calculate drought index covariates — calculate_drought
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ locs <- data.frame(site_id = "001", lon = -97.5, lat = 35.5) ## SPEI example spei <- process_drought(   source = "spei",   path = "./data/drought",   date = c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"),   timescale = 1L ) calculate_drought(   from = spei,   locs = locs,   locs_id = "site_id",   radius = 0L,   fun = "mean" ) ## USDM example usdm <- process_drought(   source = "usdm",   path = "./data/drought",   date = c("2020-01-07", "2020-03-31") ) calculate_drought(   from = usdm,   locs = locs,   locs_id = "site_id" ) } # }

title: Calculate ecoregions covariates — calculate_ecoregion

title: Calculate ecoregions covariates — calculate_ecoregion
code: calculate_ecoregion(   from = NULL,   locs,   locs_id = "site_id",   colnames = c("coded", "full_ecoregion"),   frac = FALSE,   drop = FALSE,   weights = NULL,   geom = FALSE,   radius = 0,   ... )

title: Calculate ecoregions covariates — calculate_ecoregion
text: SpatVector(1). Output process_ecoregion. locs sf/SpatVector. Unique locs. include unique identifier field named locs_id locs_id character(1). Name unique identifier. colnames character(1). Naming convention ecoregion indicator columns. Default "coded" existing numeric key-based names. Use "full_ecoregion" emit sanitized full ecoregion names. frac logical(1). Default FALSE. FALSE, returns binary dummy indicators (0/1). TRUE, returns fractional overlap values. drop logical(1). Default FALSE. TRUE, remove ecoregion columns 0 NA across returned locations. weights NULL, SpatRaster, polygon SpatVector/sf, file path. Optional weights raster weighted extraction. NULL (default), unweighted extraction performed. geom FALSE/"sf"/"terra".. function return geometry? Default FALSE, options geometry "sf" "terra". coordinate reference system sf SpatVector . radius numeric(1). Circular buffer size (meters) around point locations. Use 0 (default) exact point extraction. ... Placeholders.

title: Calculate ecoregions covariates — calculate_ecoregion
text: data.frame SpatVector object ecoregion indicator/fraction variables attributes : Indicator names controlled colnames: "coded" (default) creates key-based names DUM_E2083_00000 DUM_E3064_00000 frac = FALSE, FRC_E2083_00000 FRC_E3064_00000 frac = TRUE; "full_ecoregion" creates sanitized name-based columns DUM_E2_SOUTHEASTERN_USA_PLAINS_00000 / FRC_E2_SOUTHEASTERN_USA_PLAINS_00000 DUM_E3_NORTHERN_PIEDMONT_00000 / FRC_E3_NORTHERN_PIEDMONT_00000 (duplicates suffixed, e.g. _1). attr(., "ecoregion2_code"): Ecoregion lv.2 code key attr(., "ecoregion3_code"): Ecoregion lv.3 code key

text: character, SpatRaster, SpatVector. title: Download air quality data — download_aqs

Year start/end years downloading data. url_aqs_download character(1). URL AQS pre-generated datasets. directory_to_save character(1). Directory save data. acknowledgement logical(1). Must TRUE proceed. download logical(1). DEPRECATED. Downloads happen automatically. remove_command logical(1). Deprecated, ignored. unzip logical(1). Unzip zip files (default TRUE). remove_zip logical(1). Remove zip files unzipping (default FALSE). show_progress logical(1). Show download progress (default TRUE) hash logical(1). title: Download drought index data — download_drought
text: download_drought() function downloads drought index data publicly available sources. Three source datasets supported: SPEI (Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index): Multi-year netCDF files timescale https://spei.csic.es. EDDI (Evaporative Demand Drought Index): Weekly raster files timescale ftp://ftp.cdc.noaa.gov/Projects/EDDI/CONUS_archive/data. USDM (U.S. Drought Monitor): Weekly drought class shapefiles https://droughtmonitor.unl.edu.

title: Download drought index data — download_drought
code: download_drought(   source = c("spei", "eddi", "usdm"),   date = c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"),   timescale = 1L,   directory_to_save = NULL,   acknowledgement = FALSE,   hash = FALSE,   show_progress = TRUE,   max_tries = 3L,   rate_limit = 2,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE,   ... )

title: Download drought index data — download_drought
text: source character(1). Drought data source. One "spei", "eddi", "usdm". date character(1 2). Single date start/end dates. Format "YYYY-MM-DD". SPEI/EDDI year component selects annual file(s); USDM full date used select weekly release(s). timescale integer(1). Accumulation timescale months (SPEI/EDDI ; ignored USDM). Typical values 1, 3, 6, 12, 24, 48. Default 1L. directory_to_save character(1). Directory save downloaded data. acknowledgement logical(1). Must TRUE proceed. hash logical(1). Return rlang::hash_file() hash downloaded files. Default FALSE. show_progress logical(1). Show download progress bar. Default TRUE. max_tries integer(1). Maximum retry attempts. Default 3L. rate_limit numeric(1). Minimum seconds HTTP requests. Default 2. unzip logical(1). Unzip downloaded zip archives (USDM ). Default TRUE. remove_zip logical(1). Remove zip archives unzipping (USDM ). Default FALSE. ... Reserved future use; currently ignored.

title: Download drought index data — download_drought
text: invisible(NULL) hash = FALSE; character hash string hash = TRUE.

title: Download drought index data — download_drought

title: Download EDGAR Emissions Data — download_edgar
text: Constructs optionally downloads EDGAR emissions data URLs based user-specified inputs including species, temporal resolution, emission sectors, file formats.

title: Download EDGAR Emissions Data — download_edgar
code: download_edgar(   species = c("BC", "CO", "NH3", "NMVOC", "NOx", "OC", "PM10", "PM2.5", "SO2"),   version = "8.1",   temp_res = NULL,   sector_yearly = NULL,   sector_monthly = NULL,   sector_voc = NULL,   format = "nc",   output = "emi",   year_range = NULL,   voc = NULL,   directory_to_save = NULL,   acknowledgement = FALSE,   download = TRUE,   remove_command = FALSE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE,   hash = FALSE,   show_progress = TRUE,   max_tries = 20,   rate_limit = 2 )

title: Download EDGAR Emissions Data — download_edgar
text: species Character vector. One species download. Supported values: "BC", "CO", "NH3", "NMVOC", "NOx", "OC", "PM10", "PM2.5", "SO2". Input case-insensitive supports "pm2.5" "pm25". version Character. EDGAR data version. Supported values: "8.1" recent version data "8.1_voc" VOC speciation data. temp_res Character. Temporal resolution specification version 8.1. One "yearly", "monthly", "timeseries". temp_res needed version=8.1_voc ignored specified. sector_yearly Character vector NULL. Emission sectors yearly data. NULL, totals used. Possible values include: "AGS", "AWB", "CHE", "ENE", "IND", "MNM", "NMM", "PRU_SOL", "RCO", "REF_TRF", "SWD_INC", "SWD_LDF", "TNR_Aviation_CDS", "TNR_Aviation_CRS", "TNR_Aviation_LTO", "TNR_Aviation_SPS", "TNR_Other", "TNR_Ship", "TRO", "WWT" sector_monthly Character vector NULL. Emission sectors monthly data. NULL, function use full-species files (sector-specific). Supported values: "AGRICULTURE", "BUILDINGS", "FUEL_EXPLOITATION", "IND_COMBUSTION", "IND_PROCESSES", "POWER_INDUSTRY", "TRANSPORT", "WASTE". sector_voc Character vector NULL. Emission sectors VOC speciation data. NULL, function use full-species files (sector-specific). Supported values: "AGRICULTURE", "BUILDINGS", "FUEL_EXPLOITATION", "IND_COMBUSTION", "IND_PROCESSES", "POWER_INDUSTRY", "TRANSPORT", "WASTE". format Character. File format download. Typically "nc" (NetCDF) "txt". Flux output monthly outputs supported .nc format output Character. Output type. Supported values include "emi" emissions "flx" fluxes. year_range Numeric vector length 1, 2 NULL. Year range, e.g., 2021, c(2021, 2022). NULL, uses available years (1970-2022 yearly data, 2000-2022 monthly VOC speciation data) voc Integer vector NULL. Used VOC speciation version "8.1_voc". Accepts integers 1 25. See: https://edgar.jrc.ec.europa.eu/dataset_ap81_VOC_spec#p3  reference speciation groups VOC numbers. directory_to_save character(1). Directory save data. Two sub-directories created downloaded zip files ("/zip_files") unzipped data files ("/data_files"). acknowledgement logical(1). setting TRUE user acknowledges data downloaded using function may large use lots machine storage memory. download logical(1). FALSE generate *.txt file containing download commands. setting TRUE function download requested data files. remove_command logical(1). Remove (TRUE) keep (FALSE) text file containing download commands. Default FALSE. unzip logical(1). Unzip zip files. Default TRUE. remove_zip logical(1). Remove zip file directory_to_download. Default FALSE. hash logical(1). setting TRUE function return rlang::hash_file() hash character corresponding downloaded files. Default FALSE. show_progress logical(1). Show download progress. Default TRUE. max_tries integer(1). Maximum download retry attempts. Default 20. rate_limit numeric(1). Minimum seconds requests. Default 2.

title: Download EDGAR Emissions Data — download_edgar
text: list download URLs (character). Optionally downloads available files warns missing ones. hash = FALSE, NULL hash = TRUE, rlang::hash_file character. Zip /data files downloaded stored directory_to_save.

title: Download PRISM data — download_prism
code: download_prism(   time,   element = c("ppt", "tmin", "tmax", "tmean", "tdmean", "vpdmin", "vpdmax", "solslope",     "soltotal", "solclear", "soltrans"),   data_type = c("ts", "normals_800", "normals"),   format = c("nc", "asc", "grib2"),   directory_to_save = NULL,   acknowledgement = FALSE,   download = TRUE,   remove_command = FALSE,   unzip = TRUE,   remove_zip = FALSE,   hash = FALSE,   show_progress = TRUE,   max_tries = 20,   rate_limit = 2 )

title: Download PRISM data — download_prism
text: time character(1). Length 2, 4, 6, 8. Time period time series normals. According PRISM Web Service Guide, acceptable formats include (disclaimer: following direct quote; minimal formatting applied): Time Series: YYYYMMDD daily data (yesterday January 1st, 1981) – returns single grid .zip file YYYYMM monthly data (last month January 1981) – returns single grid .zip file YYYY annual data (last year 1981) - returns single grid .zip file YYYY historical data (1980 1895) - returns single zip file containing 12 monthly grids YYYY plus annual. Normals: Monthly normal: date MM (.e., 04 April) value 14, returns annual normal Daily normal: date MMDD (.e., 0430 April 30) element character(1). Data element. One c("ppt", "tmin", "tmax", "tmean", "tdmean", "vpdmin", "vpdmax") normals, c("solslope", "soltotal", "solclear", "soltrans") also accepted. data_type character(1). Data type. "ts": 4km resolution time series. "normals_800": 800m resolution normals. "normals": 4km resolution normals. format character(1). Data format. applicable data_type = "ts". directory_to_save character(1). Directory download files. acknowledgement logical(1). setting TRUE user acknowledges data downloaded using function may large use lots machine storage memory. download logical(1). FALSE generate *.txt file containing download commands. setting TRUE function download requested data files. remove_command logical(1). Remove (TRUE) keep (FALSE) text file containing download commands. unzip logical(1). Unzip downloaded zip file extract data files (nc, grib2, etc.) directory_to_save. Default TRUE. PRISM API always returns zip regardless requested format. remove_zip logical(1). Remove zip file unzipping. Default FALSE. applies unzip = TRUE. hash logical(1). setting TRUE function return rlang::hash_file() hash character corresponding downloaded files. Default FALSE. show_progress logical(1). Show download progress. Default TRUE. max_tries integer(1). Maximum download retry attempts. Default 20. rate_limit numeric(1). Minimum seconds requests. Default 2.

title: Download PRISM data — download_prism
text: hash = FALSE, NULL hash = TRUE, rlang::hash_file character. .bil (normals) single grid files depending format choice stored directory_to_save.

title: Download files using httr2 — download_run_method
text: Execute downloads using httr2 robust retry logic rate limiting. function handles authentication, retries, progress tracking, streams files directly disk. HTTP-status retries use exponential backoff capped 30 s avoid long hangs DNS timeouts (attempt takes ~10 s). Transport-level failures (SSL drops, connection resets) also retried max_tries times.

title: Download files using httr2 — download_run_method
code: download_run_method(   urls = NULL,   destfiles = NULL,   token = NULL,   show_progress = TRUE,   max_tries = 20,   rate_limit = 2,   timeout = 3600,   http_version = NULL )

title: Download files using httr2 — download_run_method
text: urls character vector. URLs download destfiles character vector. Destination file paths (length urls) token character(1). Authentication token (optional, e.g., NASA EarthData) show_progress logical(1). Show download progress bars (default TRUE) max_tries integer(1). Maximum number retry attempts (default 20) rate_limit numeric(1). Minimum seconds requests (default 2) timeout numeric(1). Timeout seconds request (default 3600 = 1 hour) http_version integer(1). Force HTTP version via curl's CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION: 1L = HTTP/1.0, 2L = HTTP/1.1, 3L = HTTP/2. NULL (default) lets curl negotiate automatically. Pass 2L servers drop HTTP/2 connections (e.g., www.mrlc.gov NLCD).

title: Convert spatial extent to MODIS sinusoidal tile codes — extent_to_modis_tiles
text: extent numeric(4). Bounding box c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) decimal degrees (EPSG:4326).

title: Convert spatial extent to MODIS sinusoidal tile codes — extent_to_modis_tiles
text: character vector tile codes "hXXvYY" format, ordered increasing v h.

title: Convert spatial extent to MODIS sinusoidal tile codes — extent_to_modis_tiles
text: MODIS sinusoidal grid divides globe 18 x 36 tiles, nominally covering 10 degrees latitude. sinusoidal projection compresses longitude high latitudes, geographic lon/lat bounding boxes tiles simple 10-degree squares — can significantly wider geographic longitude near poles. function uses official NASA MODLAND sinusoidal tile bounding coordinates table (sn_bound_10deg.txt, https://modis-land.gsfc.nasa.gov/pdf/sn_bound_10deg.txt) bundled inst/extdata/. returns every non-fill tile whose geographic bounding box overlaps requested extent. Horizontal tile numbers (h) range 0 35 (west east); vertical tile numbers (v) range 0 17 (north south).

title: Convert spatial extent to MODIS sinusoidal tile codes — extent_to_modis_tiles
text: Kyle Messier

USDM — returns SpatVector (polygon) columns DM (drought-monitor class, integer 0–4), date (Date), source ("usdm"), CRS EPSG:4326.

title: Process drought index data — process_drought
text: source character(1). Drought data source. One "spei", "eddi", "usdm". called process_covariates(covariate = "spei") alias forwarded automatically. path character(1). Directory containing downloaded drought files (output download_drought()). date character(1 2). Single date start/end dates. Format "YYYY-MM-DD". timescale integer(1). Accumulation timescale months (SPEI/EDDI ; ignored USDM). Must match timescale used download_drought(). Default 1L. extent numeric(4) SpatExtent. Optional spatial crop applied returning. NULL (default) returns full extent. ... Reserved future use; currently ignored.

text: SpatRaster SPEI EDDI sources. SpatVector (polygons) USDM source.

USDM files expected weekly shapefiles named USDM_<YYYYMMDD>.shp. Layer/column naming standardised calculate_drought() can operate identically regardless source.

Collection-specific variable names accepted variable: variable matching case-insensitive (example, "o3" matches "O3"). Reference: NASA GEOS-CF OpenDAP catalog https://opendap.nccs.nasa.gov/dods/gmao/geos-cf/assim.

what: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
text: process_improve() function reads pipe-delimited IMPROVE (Interagency Monitoring Protected Visual Environments) measurement files downloaded download_improve() joins site metadata table attach geographic coordinates auxiliary site attributes. Returns SpatVector, sf, data.table object.

title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
code: process_improve(   path = NULL,   product = c("raw", "rhr2", "rhr3"),   date = NULL,   sites_file = NULL,   return_format = c("terra", "sf", "data.table"),   extent = NULL,   ... )

title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
text: path character(1). Directory containing downloaded IMPROVE .txt files. product character(1). Product type: "raw" (default), "rhr2", "rhr3". date character(1 2). Date ("YYYY-MM-DD") start/end date pair filter measurements. Defaults filtering NULL. sites_file character(1) NULL. Path site metadata file. NULL (default), function first looks file named improve_sites.txt inside path, falls back embedded IMPROVE aerosol site table included amadeus. return_format character(1). Return object type: "terra", "sf", "data.table". extent numeric(4) NULL. Optional crop extent c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) WGS84 / EPSG:4326. Applied return_format "terra" "sf". ... Placeholders.

title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
text: SpatVector, sf, data.table object depending return_format.

title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
text: Three product types supported via product: "raw" IMPAER speciated aerosol mass concentrations. Key columns: SiteCode, FactDate, ParamCode, FactValue, Units. "rhr2" IMPRHR2 Regional Haze Rule II light extinction (bext, \(Mm^{-1}\)). "rhr3" IMPRHR3 Regional Haze Rule III deciview index (dv). Measurement values filtered Status; callers may apply validity flags (e.g., keep Status == "V0").

title: Process IMPROVE aerosol monitoring data — process_improve
text: IMPROVE data measured every-third-day sampling schedule. Gaps measurement dates expected.

code: process_tri(   path = NULL,   year = 2018,   variables = "STACK_AIR",   chemical = NULL,   industry_group = c("none", "industry_sector", "industry_sector_code", "both"),   ignore_case = TRUE,   extent = NULL,   ... )

title: Process toxic release data — process_tri
text: path character(1). Path directory TRI CSV files year integer(1). Single year select. variables character. One regular expressions used select TRI release variables column name normalization underscore naming (example, STACK_AIR, FUGITIVE_AIR, WATER). Default "STACK_AIR". Matching first uses raw TRI column names, falls back normalized match punctuation spaces converted underscores (example, "-SITE RELEASE TOTAL" matches ON_SITE_RELEASE_TOTAL). Recommended options include: FUGITIVE_AIR STACK_AIR WATER UNDERGROUND UNDERGROUND_CL_I UNDERGROUND_C_II_V LANDFILLS RCRA_C_LANDFILL OTHER_LANDFILLS LAND_TREATMENT SURFACE_IMPNDMNT RCRA_SURFACE_IM OTHER_SURFACE_I OTHER_DISPOSAL ON_SITE_RELEASE_TOTAL POTW_TRNS_RLSE POTW_TRNS_TRT POTW_TOTAL_TRANSFERS chemical NULL character. Optional one regular expressions used filter chemicals. Patterns matched TRI_CHEMICAL_COMPOUND_ID, CHEMICAL, CAS/CAS. values. NULL (default), chemicals retained. industry_group character(1). Optional additional grouping level. One "none" (default), "industry_sector", "industry_sector_code", "". ignore_case logical(1). TRUE (default), regular expression matching variables chemical case-insensitive. extent numeric(4) SpatExtent giving extent raster NULL (default), entire raster loaded ... Placeholders.

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
code: sum_edc(   from = NULL,   locs = NULL,   locs_id = NULL,   decay_range = NULL,   target_fields = NULL,   C0 = NULL,   use_threshold = TRUE,   geom = FALSE )

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
text: SpatVector(1). Point locations contain point-source covariate data. locs sf/SpatVector(1). Locations sum exponentially decaying contributions calculated. locs_id character(1). Name unique id field point_to. decay_range numeric(1). Distance source concentration reduced exp(-3) (approximately -95 %) target_fields character(varying). Field names characters. C0 NULL, character(1), numeric vector length nrow(). Optional initial source values pollutant locations. NULL (default), source values set 1. character(1), value treated column name used source values. use_threshold logical(1). TRUE (default), include source points within 5 * decay_range target location. FALSE, include source points . geom FALSE/"sf"/"terra".. function return geometry? Default FALSE, options geometry "sf" "terra". coordinate reference system sf SpatVector .

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
text: data.frame (tibble) SpatVector object input field names suffix "_sedc" sums EDC stored. Additional attributes attached EDC information. `attr(result, "decay_range")“: range concentration reduces approximately five percent `attr(result, "sedc_threshold")“: threshold distance emission source points excluded beyond

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
text: function originally chopin Distance calculation done terra functions internally. Thus, function internally converts sf objects point_* arguments terra. threshold carefully chosen users.

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
text: Messier KP, Akita Y, Serre ML (2012). “Integrating Address Geocoding, Land Use Regression, Spatiotemporal Geostatistical Estimation Groundwater Tetrachloroethylene.” Environmental Science & Technology, 46(5), 2772–2780. ISSN 0013-936X. doi:10.1021/es203152a . Wiesner C (????). “Euclidean Sum Exponentially Decaying Contributions Tutorial.”

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
text: Insang Song

title: Calculate isotropic Sum of Exponentially Decaying Contributions (SEDC) covariates — sum_edc
code: ## NOTE: Example is wrapped in `\dontrun{}` as function requires a large ##       amount of data which is not included in the package. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(101) ncpath <- system.file("gpkg/nc.gpkg", package = "sf") nc <- terra::vect(ncpath) nc <- terra::project(nc, "EPSG:5070") pnt_locs <- terra::centroids(nc, inside = TRUE) pnt_locs <- pnt_locs[, "NAME"] pnt_from <- terra::spatSample(nc, 10L) pnt_from$pid <- seq(1, 10) pnt_from <- pnt_from[, "pid"] pnt_from$val1 <- rgamma(10L, 1, 0.05) pnt_from$val2 <- rgamma(10L, 2, 1)  vals <- c("val1", "val2") sum_edc(pnt_locs, pnt_from, "NAME", 1e4, vals) } # }

what: Major updates to code base - breaking changes have been minmized but please report if 1.3.x versions are not working as expected

what: Spatial and Temporal summarization with .by and .by_time parameters
title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: Added ..by_time parameters calculate_*() functions consistent spatial temporal summarization options across datasets default behavior calculate_*() functions remains unchanged - .e., summarization, returning extracted summarized values temporal resolution data - users can now specify .spatial grouping (e.g., HUC, county, state) .by_time temporal grouping (e.g., year, month) obtain summarized covariate values directly calculation step

what: Detailed vignettes for each dataset including available variables, spatial and temporal resolution, and example use cases

text: workflow vignette dataset detailed information available variables, spatial temporal resolution, example use cases dataset

title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)

title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: Added support additional MODIS products focus burned area active fire products

what: Finalize data API that were previously in development
title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: Added completed functonality PRISM, EDGAR, CropScape, HUC datasets

what: Use of GitHub Copilot for code generation and refactoring
title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: Used GitHub Copilot help code generation refactoring across code base, including function development, documentation, unit tests; generated code reviewed edited development team ensure accuracy consistency package standards

title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: SPEI (Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index): multi-year netCDF files accumulation timescale https://spei.csic.es EDDI (Evaporative Demand Drought Index): weekly files accumulation timescale NOAA PSL (https://downloads.psl.noaa.gov/Projects/EDDI/CONUS_archive/data); legacy netCDF files CONUS_Archive path also supported USDM (U.S. Drought Monitor): weekly polygon shapefiles https://droughtmonitor.unl.edu three datasets accessible via top-level wrappers: download_data(), process_covariates(), calculate_covariates() using aliases "drought", "spei", "eddi", "usdm" dataset_name / covariate. process_drought() returns SpatRaster (SPEI/EDDI) SpatVector polygons (USDM), CRS EPSG:4326. calculate_drought() supports ./ .by_time post-extraction summarization consistent calculate_*() functions.

title: amadeus 2.0.0 (dev)
text: Completed migration download_* functions httr/wget/curl command-line calls httr2 network requests Deprecated download parameter (use default download = TRUE) remove_command parameter across download functions; now emit informative warnings ignored Added hash parameter download_* functions optional MD5 file integrity verification via download_hash() (using system md5sum) Added unzip remove_zip parameters download_prism() post-download archive handling Added download_run_method() internal helper unified httr2-based file retrieval progress reporting, retry logic, rate limiting Improved check_url_status() general-purpose URL validation Expanded unit test coverage mock-based tests download functions covering deprecation warnings, hash paths, file-exists branches Added full variable reference download_narr() documentation, listing 88 available NARR variable abbreviations descriptions grouped category (monolevel, pressure level, subsurface); resolves #194 Added download_narr() tests covering every variable abbreviation across three variable categories Fixed download_data() dispatch include "edgar" download_edgar() reachable via wrapper function

title: amadeus 1.3.2
text: Fixed deprecated file paths NLCD, MODIS, Ecoregions datasets Removed certificate verification ecoregion download needed anymore NASA datasets MODIS, VIIRS, Geos-CF, added additional tags wget scripts prevent API throttling httr upgraded httr2 Modified internal URL status check function general use Added news function display NEWS.md content within R pkgdown site Improved reporting GitHub Actions workflows

title: amadeus 1.2
text: nthreads argument removed calculate_modis_par() calculate_nlcd() calculate_modis_par() renamed calculate_modis()

title: amadeus 1.1
text: calc_*() functions renamed calculate_*() per naming convention function family package

title: amadeus 1.0
text: First CRAN release (v.1.0.0)
